LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT REASSURE AMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE WRIT OF MANDAMUS ALLEGATIONS FROM PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT LACKS STANDING TO DEMURRER TO A CLAIM ALLEGED AGAINST ANOTHER PARTY. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS SHOWING THAT HIS RELIANCE ON DEFENDANTS' REPRESENTATIONS ABOUT THE MEDICAL EXAMINER'S "INDEPENDENT" STATUS WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGED HARM. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-02-2011 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DEBORA CAHN-WEINER, PH.D.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED AN INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIM (SEE YOUNAN V. EQUIFAX INC. (1980) 111 CAL.APP.3D 498). PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND HER INTERFERENCE TORTS TO ALLEGE THAT DR. CAHN-WEINER WAS A "STRANGER" AND NOT AN "AGENT" INSOFAR AS THE ALLEGED TORTUOUS CONDUCT IS CONCERNED. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-02-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT DEBORA CAHN-WEINER, PH.D.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Robert D. Sanford, Esq. of Supple & Canvel LLP 366-5533 for deft & moving party; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Overruled as to the fourth cause of action and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fifth and sixth causes of action. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled an intentional infliction of emotional distress claim (See Younan v. Equifax Inc. (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 498). Plaintiff is given leave to amend her interference torts to allege that Dr. Cahn-Weiner was a "stranger" and not an "agent" insofar as the alleged tortuous conduct is concerned. Order on demurrer signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-02-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT DEBORA CAHN-WEINER, PH.D.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS FROM FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Robert D. Sanford, Esq. of Supple & Canvel LLP 366-5533 for deft & moving party; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Granted. Plaintiff's allegations against Dr. Cahn-Weiner are within the scope of CCP 425.13. Order on motion to strke signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-02-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT REASSURE AMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE WRIT OF MANDAMUS ALLEGATIONS FROM PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Overruled as to the first cause of action and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the third cause of action. Defendant lacks standing to demurrer to a claim alleged against another party. As to the third cause of action for intentional misrepresentation, plaintiff is given leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege ultimate facts showing that his reliance on defendants' representations about the medical examiner's "independent" status was a substantial factor in causing plaintiff's alleged harm. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
